pool.baseline: staring at his side of the bed and it's just... everywhere. the silence in this house is actually deafening. i don't even know why i bother waking up just to sit in this emptiness. it feels like i died with him and now i'm just a ghost wandering around a life that doesn't belong to me anymore.

pool.baseline: sometimes i just stare at the ceiling and realize it's all just... nothing. like, we're just chemical accidents on a tiny rock in a universe that is literally just expanding into a cold, dark void. in a billion years, or even just a trillion, there won't even be a trace that we ever existed. it makes everything—every struggle, every feeling, every single thing i do—feel like a complete joke. how am i supposed to care about anything when the math says nothing matters?

pool.baseline: The candles have all guttered out, and the manor is so very cold. He is gone... he has taken the very light of my soul with him into the fog. I feel like a ghost already, drifting through these empty halls. There is no more purpose in breathing when every breath only reminds me of the void he left behind. I feel as though I am simply waiting for the darkness to finally claim what is left of me.
